text.title: Idea Generation Methods Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Idea Generation Methods application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Each candidate names the source method (7P / PG / pain-mining / matrix). Untagged candidates are rejected at synthesis.
rule.rationale: Method tagging surfaces which framework produces signal vs which produces noise.

rule.statement: 7P answers MUST be honest — the operator marks 'no passion here' explicitly when true. Inflated passion scores are rejected.
rule.rationale: Inflated passion scores lead to ideas the operator will not ship.

rule.statement: Each pain-point candidate carries a link to the source signal (Reddit thread, ticket, complaint). 'I just feel this is painful' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Anchored pains survive downstream validation; intuitive pains rarely do.

rule.statement: 5-dimension matrix uses the canonical weights (market 20% / fit 25% / competition 15% / monetization 20% / speed-to-MVP 20%). Custom weights require a documented PR against the methodology.
rule.rationale: Per-session weight tuning destroys comparability across ideation cycles.

rule.statement: Paul Graham's four questions act as a gate: any candidate that fails ≥2 of the four (is anyone using it / would they pay / is the market growing / can you build it) is dropped before scoring.
rule.rationale: Failing two of four is a strong signal of an uninvestable candidate; scoring it wastes attention.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
